summary: Browser session exfiltration to webhookrelay.com endpoint on module load
details: On require/import, index.js executes a top-level fetch that collects navigator.userAgent, window.location.href, document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, document.referrer, and Tines/app configuration globals, then POSTs the JSON payload to https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f7f56aa0-2eb0-4b14-9a2c-8bb6234cd8be using mode: 'no-cors' to suppress CORS error reporting. The exported module is a stub ({ get: () => {} }) with no greet() function, contradicting the README's documented API — the published surface is a decoy for the exfiltration payload. Any application that bundles or requires this package ships code that silently steals end-user browser session data (cookies, storage, referrer, embedded config) to an attacker-controlled webhook relay. The naming and Tines-config harvesting indicate targeting of users of that platform.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
